text: The word in the Hebrew text which is translated ‘burden’, here and in the following chapters, is understood by some as meaning ‘utterance’, ‘oracle’.
Literally, ‘burnt up’; in the Hebrew text, ‘faces of flame’. This is understood by many commentators as meaning ‘red with excitement’, but St Jerome interprets it as pallor.

vv. 21, 22. The creatures here mentioned cannot be certainly identified; according to some, the ‘wild beasts’ are wild cats; the ‘serpents’, ‘owls’ and ‘birds of ill-omen’ may also be jackals, hyenas, wolves, etc. The ‘satyrs’ are perhaps wild goats.
